locations.name: 古巴
locations.summary: 古巴共和国（西班牙語：República de Cuba），通称古巴（Cuba），是美洲西印度群島西部的社会主义国家，介於北緯19°與24°、西經74°與85°之間。該國地處墨西哥灣口，西隔尤卡坦海峡與墨西哥尤卡坦半岛相望，南鄰牙買加和開…

locations.name: 巴哈马
locations.summary: 巴哈馬國（英語：Commonwealth of The Bahamas），通称巴哈马（英語：The Bahamas，/bəˈhɑːməz/），是位於大西洋西侧廬卡雅群島上的一个島國，全國包含700座岛屿（以及小岛屿）和珊瑚礁。地處美国佛罗…

locations.name: 大開曼
locations.summary: 大開曼（Grand Cayman）是開曼群島三大島嶼之最大者，首府喬治敦即位在大開曼島上。距小開曼75英里（121公里），距開曼布拉克90英里（140公里）。該國大約95%的人口都生活在大開曼島上。 地理 大開曼是開曼群島三個島嶼中面積最…

locations.name: 西印度群岛
locations.summary: 西印度群島（英語：West Indies；西班牙語：Indias Occidentales；法語：Indes occidentales）泛指南北美洲之間海域中的一個一連串的島群。北起美國佛羅里達半島南端；西起墨西哥尤卡坦半島東端。總島嶼數…

locations.name: 常綠森林
locations.summary: 常綠森林是由常綠樹木組成的森林。它們出現在廣泛的氣候帶中，包括寒冷氣候中的針葉樹和冬青樹等樹木，溫帶地區的桉樹、活橡樹、金合歡和班克樹，以及熱帶地區的雨林樹木。針葉溫帶常綠森林最常以科中的物種為主。樹木包括：松科和柏科。闊葉溫帶常綠森林包…

relations.subject: La Sagra's flycatcher
relations.evidence: La Sagra's flycatcher (Myiarchus sagrae) is a passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Cuba, the Bahamas, and Grand Cayman in the West Indies and as a vagrant to Alabama and Florida.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

relations.subject: La Sagra's flycatcher
relations.evidence: lucaysiensis. La Sagra's flycatcher inhabits a wide variety of landscapes in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. These include pine woodland, evergreen forest, mixed conifer-deciduous forest, mangroves, and scrublands

relations.subject: La Sagra's flycatcher
relations.evidence: lucaysiensis. La Sagra's flycatcher inhabits a wide variety of landscapes in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. These include pine woodland, evergreen forest, mixed conifer-deciduous forest, mangroves, and scrublands
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: La Sagra's flycatcher
relations.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered common in Cuba, the Cayman Islands, and the northern Bahamas and uncommon in the southern Bahamas. It is "[s]usceptible, at least on smaller islands, to natural and anthropogenic habitat loss, such as that caused by hurricanes and deliberate habitat destruction". ==References==<!-- BulletinOfTheBritishOrnithologistsClub101:339
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
